description: Since this morning I cannot access my account. Password reset email never received. I have a payment due tonight.
ai_summary: User unable to login; password reset email not received, payment due tonight
ai_reasoning: This ticket clearly describes an access issue with multiple compounding factors: login failure, non-receipt of password reset email, and an imminent payment deadline. The combination of authentication problems and time-sensitive financial obligation justifies High priority classification. The confidence score of 0.92 reflects the unambiguous nature of the access category and the clear urgency indicators. Escalation to a human agent is recommended to expedite resolution and prevent potential service disruption and missed payment consequences.

description: I would like to update my delivery address for my next order before it ships tomorrow.
ai_summary: Customer requests delivery address update for order shipping tomorrow
ai_reasoning: This ticket does not clearly fit the CCaaS tools specialist domain (Odigo, Ring Central, SightCall, etc.) as it appears to be a general customer service request related to order management rather than communication platform issues. The request is straightforward with a time-sensitive element (shipping tomorrow), warranting Medium priority. However, confidence is below 0.7 due to ambiguity about whether this ticket should be routed to the CCaaS support team at all, as it seems to be a general e-commerce/logistics inquiry. The low confidence triggers escalation consideration, but given the routine nature and clear resolution path, escalation is not strictly necessary if proper routing procedures are in place.

description: Your application keeps crashing every time I try to open it. This has been happening for 3 days. I will cancel my subscription if not fixed.
ai_summary: Application crashes on login screen for 3 days; user threatens subscription cancellation
ai_reasoning: This ticket clearly describes a technical issue with the application crashing on the login screen, persisting for 3 days. The high priority is justified by: (1) the critical nature of the bug preventing user access, (2) the extended duration indicating a systemic problem, and (3) the explicit threat of subscription cancellation indicating high customer dissatisfaction and churn risk. The confidence score of 0.92 reflects the unambiguous technical classification and clear high-priority indicators. Escalation is recommended due to the churn risk and need for immediate investigation and resolution.

description: My last invoice shows 150 EUR but I should have been charged 99 EUR according to my plan. Please correct this urgently.
ai_summary: Invoice amount discrepancy: charged 150 EUR instead of contracted 99 EUR rate.
ai_reasoning: This ticket clearly falls into the Billing category with a specific monetary discrepancy (150 EUR vs 99 EUR). The issue is unambiguous and quantifiable, warranting a High priority due to financial impact and the reporter's urgent tone. The confidence score of 0.92 reflects the clear nature of the billing dispute. Escalation is recommended despite high confidence because billing discrepancies require human verification and correction authority, and the financial impact justifies immediate attention to prevent customer dissatisfaction.

description: It would be great to have a dark mode option in the settings. Many users have been requesting this.
ai_summary: User requests dark mode feature implementation for the application settings.
ai_reasoning: This ticket is clearly a feature request rather than a support issue related to access, billing, or technical problems. The request is non-urgent and does not impact current system functionality or user operations. While the reporter mentions multiple user requests, there is no indication of system failure, payment issues, or access problems. The low priority and 'Other' category classification are appropriate as this is a product enhancement suggestion. The high confidence score (0.92) reflects the unambiguous nature of this being a feature request with no urgency or escalation requirements.
